summary: Corrected the GALE01 symbol map so the function at .text:0x8038E034 is named HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize instead of the placeholder-style AXDriver_8038E034. This was a minimal config-only rename in config/GALE01/symbols.txt with no code changes or review discussion in the provided slice.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; updates the symbol name for .text:0x8038E034 from AXDriver_8038E034 to HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize.

what_changed: In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, renamed AXDriver_8038E034 to HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize.
Kept the same address, section, type, size, and scope metadata: .text:0x8038E034, type:function, size:0x2D8, scope:global.
No source implementation, header, or assembly changes are shown in the PR slice.
smart_moves: Performed the correction as a one-line symbol-map change, avoiding unnecessary churn around adjacent AXDriver audio symbols.
Preserved all symbol metadata while changing only the identifier, reducing risk of accidental address or size drift.
Replaced an address-derived placeholder name with a semantic HSD audio API-style name.
decomp_lessons: When a function has a confirmed semantic name, update the symbol map directly while preserving address and metadata exactly.
Small symbol-only PRs are useful for correcting naming state without touching matched code or unrelated neighboring symbols.
Address-based placeholders such as AXDriver_8038E034 should be replaced by descriptive names once the function identity is known.

naming_conventions.lesson: Uses the HSD_ subsystem prefix and an Audio verb phrase for a global audio-related function.
Address-suffixed AXDriver names in the symbol map indicate less-specific placeholder names that may later be replaced by confirmed semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic is evidenced beyond maintaining the same .text address and function size during the rename.
The stable address .text:0x8038E034 and unchanged size 0x2D8 indicate this was a naming correction, not a code-generation or matching change.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add declarations or source references for HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize.
Search for remaining AXDriver_<address> placeholders near 0x8038DA70-0x8038E498 that may have known HSD_Audio names.
Check whether HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize appears in headers, map files, or external debug symbols to validate the naming source.
